title: Day 1 · Hello Agent
subtitle: A compact map of today's core agent logic: model proposes, harness executes, observation returns to messages.

diagrams.label: Main loop
diagrams.title: The minimum Agent Loop
diagrams.summary: The prompt enters messages, MockProvider proposes a tool_call, ToolRegistry runs echo, ToolResult returns as observation, and the model produces final text.

nodes.label: CLI Runtime
nodes.detail: CLI Runtime catches user input, resolves the working directory boundary, and passes the prompt into run_agent. It does not answer the user itself.

nodes.label: messages
nodes.detail: messages is the context sent back to the model each round. Day 1 starts with a role='user' prompt, then appends the tool observation.

nodes.label: MockProvider
nodes.detail: MockProvider is the teaching fake model. On the first round it sees a user message and returns a tool_call; on the second round it sees a tool message and returns final text.

nodes.label: ModelResponse
nodes.detail: The model only proposes tool_call: echo(text='hi'). Nothing has changed in the world yet; the intent is handed to the harness.

nodes.label: ToolRegistry
nodes.detail: The model proposes `tool_call`; the harness executes the tool. ToolRegistry maps the tool name to a Python function, which is the first important boundary in Day 1.

nodes.label: ToolResult
nodes.detail: ToolResult is the observation from tool execution. It carries the tool_call_id back into messages so the model can see what echo returned.

nodes.label: stdout
nodes.detail: After the second complete(messages), MockProvider returns final text. The CLI prints it to the terminal and the Agent Loop exits.

diagrams.label: Message shape
diagrams.title: Day 1 message shape
diagrams.summary: Day 1 uses a simplified role='tool' shape to make the mock loop run. Day 2 swaps this for Anthropic tool_use / tool_result content blocks.

nodes.label: user prompt
nodes.detail: The user input is still natural language. The CLI does not parse tool arguments; it places the raw prompt into messages.

nodes.label: messages[0]
nodes.detail: run_agent initializes messages = [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }]. This is the first context the model sees.

nodes.label: ModelResponse
nodes.detail: MockProvider returns ModelResponse(tool_calls=[ToolCall(name='echo')]). This means the model wants the harness to execute a tool.

nodes.label: ToolRegistry.run
nodes.detail: ToolRegistry.run(call) looks up the echo function by call.name and passes call.arguments into it.

nodes.label: messages += tool
nodes.detail: This is the Day 1 teaching simplification: role='tool' stores the ToolResult. Day 2 changes this to the Anthropic Messages API tool_use / tool_result content blocks.

nodes.label: ModelResponse
nodes.detail: MockProvider sees that the newest message is a tool observation, then returns final text with stop_reason back to end_turn.

nodes.label: stdout
nodes.detail: The CLI renders the trace: tool_call, observation, final. The Terminal Replay later in the lesson still shows this runnable output.
